title: Server-state query/cache lifecycle — placement of the keyed staleness-aware cache contract
summary: Prior-art survey grounding decision #1419 (surfaced by the data-lifecycle lens #1403): the server-state cache lifecycle that TanStack Query / SWR / RTK Query / Apollo all converged on — a keyed query cache with staleness/TTL, in-flight de-dupe, invalidate/refresh, background-refetch, and query dependency — has no WE owner. It is the read-path counterpart to optimistic mutation (#1395, prepared). Native grounding: the platform standardizes this at the HTTP layer — Cache-Control (RFC 9111 + RFC 5861): max-age=N is the FRESH WINDOW (TanStack staleTime), after which the entry is stale-but-usable; stale-while-revalidate=N serves stale immediately while revalidating in the background (the exact SWR pattern, named at the protocol level); stale-if-error maps to the reliability failure seam. Cache API/CacheStorage is the platform's keyed request->response store (the natural model for a swappable cache PROVIDER); AbortController/AbortSignal is the dedupe cancellation primitive resource-loader already uses. Vocabulary to standardize on: query key, fresh vs stale + fresh window (max-age/staleTime), revalidate (prefer the RFC verb over 'refetch'), stale-while-revalidate, evict/garbage-collect (gcTime), invalidate, dependent query, fetch policy (cache-first/network-only/cache-and-network), in-flight dedupe, normalized cache. Load-bearing finding: four libraries converged on ONE model with different vocabulary (the #1405 divergence-pass insight) — shared concept -> {cache key: queryKey/key/endpoint+args/normalized ID; fresh window: staleTime/dedupe window; unused-entry GC: gcTime/keepUnusedDataFor/cache.gc(); in-flight dedupe: by key/dedupingInterval; invalidate: invalidateQueries/mutate(key)/invalidatesTags/evict; background revalidate: on focus/reconnect/subscription/cache-and-network; dependent query: enabled/conditional key/skip}. The model: a keyed store of {data, status, freshUntil} entries, with de-duped in-flight fetches, staleness-driven background revalidation, on-demand invalidation, and unused-entry GC — same model, four vocabularies = textbook intent-abstraction. Finding 2: the fresh/stale split is two INDEPENDENT timers, both platform-named (staleTime/max-age = when to revalidate, orthogonal to gcTime/keepUnusedDataFor = when to evict); SWR is literally named after stale-while-revalidate (RFC 5861) so native-first alignment is the lineage. Finding 3: invalidation is the read<->write seam, already half-pointed-at — resource-action.json already lists invalidateQueries as a TanStack pattern it mirrors but has NO cache to invalidate, confirming the residual is a real named gap the write side points at (#1395 is the prepared write-path decision; this is its read-path counterpart). WE-tree: WE owns one-fetch UI state, NOT a cross-query cache — loader.json is pending-state UX profiles (its 'stale' state = showing previous data during a refresh, a per-operation version token race guard, NO key, NO cross-query store); resource-loader.json is the single-fetch orchestrator (withStaleWhileRevalidate keeps previously-rendered content visible during ONE refresh; cites staleTime/gcTime/dedupe in a framework comparison but does NOT cache across calls); prefetch.json is eagerness only; simple-store.json is generic reactive CLIENT-state (no keys/staleness/fetch). No query/server-state/cache intent or block exists. Precise unowned residual: a shared, keyed, staleness-aware cache ACROSS queries — key -> {data, freshUntil, status, revalidate()} — with in-flight dedupe, background revalidation, on-demand invalidation, dependent-query gating, unused-entry GC. Recommendation: (Fork 1, ~85%) mint a first-class query (server-state) INTENT abstracting key -> {data, staleness, revalidate} over a swappable cache provider — extending resource-loader/loader is the BROKEN branch (those model one fetch's UI state; a cross-query shared keyed store is a store not a fetch; the symmetric mistake #1395 untangled on the write side where loader.strategy.optimistic wrongly hosted the write lifecycle); dimensions borrow platform vocabulary (fetchPolicy, freshWindow/staleTime, evictAfter/gcTime, revalidateOn, dedupe, dependsOn). (Fork 1-sub, ~85%) the cache is a swappable runtime-DI PROVIDER behind the contract (baking one impl is broken — forecloses the Cache API / in-memory map / normalized entity store, violates protocol-is-the-only-lock; the key->{data,staleness,revalidate} interface is the lock); normalized caching is an optional provider capability, not mandated. Relationship to #1395 (supported, not a fork): the read-path symmetric counterpart to #1395's prepared write-path mutation decision — they compose (a mutation invalidates query cache entries and optimistically writes a predicted result into the cache), completing a clean three-way loader (single-fetch UI) / query (cross-query cache, reads) / proposed mutation lifecycle (writes); follow #1395's precedent (new intent + retarget an existing FUI block, not greenfield). Supported by default: the running cache stays in FUI (a resource-cache block or resource-loader extension); query composes loader for per-entry display states; fetch-policy is an author dimension, most-permissive default cache-and-network (the stale-while-revalidate default the platform blesses); prefetch composes query; dependent queries = a dependsOn/enabled gate; normalized caching = optional provider capability. Report: reports/2026-06-21-server-state-cache-lifecycle.md.
